channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Old-school digicams are everywhere. Why?
item.description: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for others, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.content:encoded: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for others, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Trump relished in being compared to dictators like Hitler and Stalin, journalist says
item.description: The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: When life is chaos, make art
item.description: Artists create, even during times of chaos. In response to Trump policies and ICE's incursions, arts organizations are leaning in to new ways of fostering community in ways large and small.
item.content:encoded:
Artists create, even during times of chaos. In response to Trump policies and ICE's incursions, arts organizations are leaning in to new ways of fostering community in ways large and small.

(Image credit: Olivia Obineme for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Too weak to advocate for herself, a young woman is saved by a nun
item.description: When Solitaire Miles was 18, she had a stroke. When she got to the hospital, doctors and nurses accused her of being on drugs. Then, her unsung hero arrived and ensured she got the care she needed.
item.content:encoded:
When Solitaire Miles was 18, she had a stroke. When she got to the hospital, doctors and nurses accused her of being on drugs. Then, her unsung hero arrived and ensured she got the care she needed.

(Image credit: Solitaire Miles)
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: Bill Maher on comedy, atheism and dining with Trump
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with comedian Bill Maher about what drives his ideas and what it means to win the Mark Twain prize for American Humor.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with comedian Bill Maher about what drives his ideas and what it means to win the Mark Twain prize for American Humor.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Harry Dean Stanton fans celebrate the character actor with a festival in Kentucky
item.description: Character actor Harry Dean Stanton would be 100 years old this week and fans in his home state of Kentucky still celebrate him each year with a festival.
item.content:encoded: Character actor Harry Dean Stanton would be 100 years old this week and fans in his home state of Kentucky still celebrate him each year with a festival.
item.dc:creator: Derek Operle

item.title: Some international artists are skipping U.S. tours. Others may follow suit
item.description: The visa process for visiting artists has always been complicated and expensive. Under the current administration, it's gotten significantly worse.
item.content:encoded: The visa process for visiting artists has always been complicated and expensive. Under the current administration, it's gotten significantly worse.
item.dc:creator: Josie Fischels

item.title: 'The Trojan Teddy Bear': The promise and peril of childhood in the age of AI
item.description: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.content:encoded: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Your cheat sheet to the teams headed to the World Cup semi-finals
item.description: We're at the final stretch of the World Cup. It feels like just yesterday, international fans were arriving in the U.S. and marveling at Buc-ee's and unlimited free soda.
item.content:encoded:
We're at the final stretch of the World Cup. It feels like just yesterday, international fans were arriving in the U.S. and marveling at Buc-ee's and unlimited free soda.

(Image credit: Justin Setterfield)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: New Mexico lowriders celebrate culture, push for a museum
item.description: Santa Fe is among many American communities that celebrate lowrider culture. The cars are works of art and expressions of pride. At a parade, people talk about efforts to win funding for a museum.
item.content:encoded: Santa Fe is among many American communities that celebrate lowrider culture. The cars are works of art and expressions of pride. At a parade, people talk about efforts to win funding for a museum.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton
